text: This article examines how key dimensions of international educational integration influence the reform of national vocational and higher education systems. We identify and analyze these integrative processes across several critical strata: statutory regulation, the overarching mission of higher education, organizational restructuring, quality assurance protocols, and the management of curricular content. Furthermore, the study evaluates the competitive landscape of the Russian educational market, weighing the specific benefits and risks associated with system-wide reforms. We also assess the broader economic and social impacts of hosting international students within Russian universities. These findings offer practical utility for departments of international cooperation seeking to align national schools with a unified global educational space. Additionally, the research serves as a resource for academic leadership in developing strategies and teaching aids centered on the challenges of reforming professional education within the framework of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes.
